title: Today's Markets Brief
summary: A look at Social Security strategies and student loan woes

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I'm here to walk you through some of the latest headlines in the world of finance. We're going to talk about Social Security strategies and student loan concerns. Let's dive in.

And then there's the broader implications of these stories. They highlight the complexities of financial planning, right? From Social Security strategies to student loan debt, it's clear that there's no one-size-fits-all solution. It's a reminder to take a closer look at our own financial situations and make informed decisions.

My quick read: today's market story has that familiar split-screen feeling. There is real excitement around artificial intelligence and chips, but the room still keeps one eye on risk, because markets enjoy a celebration more when they know where the exits are.

sources.title: My friend, 62, earns $20,000 a year. Should she take Social Security now — and claim survivor’s benefit at 67?
My husband took out a $100,000 Parent PLUS loan for his daughter. She dropped out, citing mental-health issues. Should we refinance?

The death of Nascar driver Kyle Busch is renewing the debate around indexed universal life insurance
Your retirement plan may have a ‘blind spot’ that could leave you without enough money
